differentials.name: severe chest pain: precordial
differentials.raw: severe chest pain: precordial (ascending aorta), neck-jaw (aortic arch), interscapular (descending thoracic aorta)

differentials.name: anterior chest wall contusion, dyspnea, dysphagia
differentials.raw: anterior chest wall contusion, dyspnea, dysphagia

differentials.name: scapulothoracic syndrome = decreased / absent upper extremity pulses
differentials.raw: scapulothoracic syndrome = decreased / absent upper extremity pulses

differentials.name: acute coarctation syndrome = decreased / absent lower extremity + normal upper extremity pulses with upper extremity hypertension + systolic murmur in 2nd left parasternal interspace
differentials.raw: acute coarctation syndrome = decreased / absent lower extremity + normal upper extremity pulses with upper extremity hypertension + systolic murmur in 2nd left parasternal interspace

differentials.name: Normal well-defined mediastinal + aortic contours on CXR: no further imaging
differentials.raw: Normal well-defined mediastinal + aortic contours on CXR: no further imaging

differentials.name: Unstable patient + unequivocally abnormal CXR / strong clinical evidence of aortic injury:
differentials.raw: Unstable patient + unequivocally abnormal CXR / strong clinical evidence of aortic injury:

differentials.name: 80-90% fatal at scene of accident ◊  Mortality rises rapidly within first 24 hours
differentials.raw: 80-90% fatal at scene of accident ◊  Mortality rises rapidly within first 24 hours

differentials.name: 10-20% reach hospital ← formation of periaortic hematoma + false aneurysm contained by adventitia ± surrounding connective tissue (a)  without intervention: 30% (40-50%) dead within 6 (24) hours; 90% dead within 4 months; chronic false aneurysm may develop in 2-5% at isthmus / descending aorta (b)  with surgical repair: 60-70% survive; surgical mortality rate of 9-44% varies with degree of hemodynamic instability + severity of associated injuries + magnitude of aortic laceration
differentials.raw: 10-20% reach hospital ← formation of periaortic hematoma + false aneurysm contained by adventitia ± surrounding connective tissue (a)  without intervention: 30% (40-50%) dead within 6 (24) hours; 90% dead within 4 months; chronic false aneurysm may develop in 2-5% at isthmus / descending aorta (b)  with surgical repair: 60-70% survive; surgical mortality rate of 9-44% varies with degree of hemodynamic instability + severity of associated injuries + magnitude of aortic laceration

differentials.name: symptom-free period of months to years
differentials.raw: symptom-free period of months to years (in 11% > 10 years)

differentials.name: delayed clinical symptoms: 42%
differentials.raw: delayed clinical symptoms: 42% (85%) within 5 (20) years consisting of chest pain, back pain, dyspnea, cough, hoarseness, dysphagia, systolic murmur Location:  descending aorta at level of lig. arteriosum filling the aorticopulmonary window (most commonly)

context: Prevalence:   10-16-20% of all fatalities in high-speed deceleration accidents CXR (53-100% sensitive, 1-60% specific, 4-20% PPV): ◊  A normal anteroposterior upright CXR virtually excludes acute thoracic aortic injury (96- 98% NPV)! N.B.:   There are no plain CXR findings of aortic injury (since aortic integrity is maintained by intact adventitia)! The sources of mediastinal hematoma are frequently the azygos, hemiazygos, internal thoracic, paraspinal and intercostal vessels! ◊  Aortic injury is the cause of mediastinal hematoma in only 12.5%! √  normal admission CXR in 28% (radiographic sign
